nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## 1. Continuously Monitor Active Linear Issues
[Learn more about the GraphQL node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

To keep up with the latest changes in our active Linear tickets, we'll need to use Linear's GraphQL endpoint because filtering is currently unavailable in the official Linear.app node.

For this demonstration, we'll check for updated tickets every 30mins.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1
nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2
nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
## 4. Get Notified when Sentiment becomes Negative
[Learn more about the Slack node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

A good use-case for tracking sentiment transitions could be to be alerted if ever an issue moves from a non-negative sentiment to a negative one. This could be a signal of issue handling troubles which may require attention before it escalates.

In this demonstration, we use the Airtable trigger to catch rows which have their sentiment column updated and check for the non-negative-to-negative sentiment transition using the switch node. For those matching rows, we combine add send a notification via slack. A cool trick is to use the "remove duplication" node to prevent repeat notifications for the same updates - here we combine the Linear issue key and the row's last modified date.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!
### This n8n template performs continous monitoring on Linear Issue conversations performing sentiment analysis and alerting when the sentiment becomes negative.
This is helpful to quickly identify difficult customer support situations early and prioritising them before they get out of hand.

## How it works
* A scheduled trigger is used to fetch recently updated issues in Linear using the GraphQL node.
* Each issue's comments thread is passed into a simple Information Extractor node to identify the overall sentiment.
* The resulting sentiment analysis combined with the some issue details are uploaded to Airtable for review.
* When the template is re-run at a later date, each issue is re-analysed for sentiment
* Each issue's new sentiment state is saved to the airtable whilst its previous state is moved to the "previous sentiment" column.
* An Airtable trigger is used to watch for recently updated rows
* Each matching Airtable row is filtered to check if it has a previous non-negative state but now has a negative state in its current sentiment.
* The results are sent via notification to a team slack channel for priority.

**Check out the sample Airtable here**: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## How to use
* Modify the GraphQL filter to fetch issues to a relevant issue type, team or person.
* Update the Slack channel to ensure messages are sent to the correct location.

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
airtable Token  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Airtable Trigger".
Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Issues to List" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sentiment over Issue Comments" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "For Each Issue..." node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Existing Sentiment" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Row" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sentiment Transition" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Fetch Active Linear Issues" node (n8n-nodes-base.graphql) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Deduplicate Notifications" node (n8n-nodes-base.removeDuplicates) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Report Issue Negative Transition" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Combine Sentiment Analysis".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Copy of Issue".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
